title: ISic003501. Fragment of a Greek inscription
body:
Text encoding: Valentina Mignosa, Jonathan Prag; edition source: autopsy
1[---]ΝΙΚΑ
2[---]ΣΑ
3[---].

Text encoding: Valentina Mignosa, Jonathan Prag; edition source: autopsy
1[---]ΝΙΚΑ
2[---]ΣΑ
3[---]·

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text from autopsy;
  • 3: Omitted by Manganaro

translation

commentary

The space below the letter A in line 2 is at least 10mm, and so much more than the interlineation of lines 1-2. If the text, as seems likely, is funerary, then the single stroke visible lower right, given its relative position, could well be the tail of a concluding hedera. Manganaro publishes this on the basis of a photograph he had previously taken of the fragment in the museum stores. As such, he speculatively restores it as a reference to a victory in a contest of some sort, by someone, son of [--]σας. Given the clear evidence that the fragment is a piece of marble in later re-use, and given the number of names that end in [---]νικα, it seems far more likely that this is a fragment of a funerary inscription from the second century CE or later.

bibliography

  • 49.1331.7 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000796
  • Manganaro 1999 68-69 no.64 fig.146 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001813

Deep, simply v-cut letters, with pronounced terminal serifs.

Line 1-2 19 Interlineation line 1 to 2 5-6
